title: Roku Q1 2021 Earnings Summary
text: Roku significantly exceeded Wall Street expectations in its first quarter of 2021. The streaming platform reported revenue of $574.2 million, representing a remarkable 79% year-over-year increase. Most notably, the company posted an unexpected net income of $76.3 million (54 cents per diluted share), when analysts had projected a loss of 13 cents per share. Despite strong financial results, user growth showed signs of deceleration. Roku added 2.4 million new active accounts during Q1, bringing its total to 53.6 million—a 35% year-over-year increase. However, this quarterly growth rate lagged behind the company's 2020 performance, which had ranged between 37-43% per quarter, reflecting the cooling of pandemic-driven streaming momentum. The Platform segment, which encompasses advertising sales and content partnership revenue, doubled to $466.5 million. Streaming consumption reached new heights, with 18.3 billion hours consumed on Roku devices during the quarter. The company also reported strong monetization metrics, with average revenue per user hitting $32.14 on a trailing 12-month basis—a 32% year-over-year gain. For Q2, Roku projected revenue between $610-620 million and net income of $10-20 million. The company continues investing in original content, having acquired the Quibi library and This Old House Ventures to bolster its Roku Channel offerings.
